source: CGD-1483 testnet simulation against atlantic-2 + CGD-1465 final-report.md
_note: This is the empirical Step-6 whitelist derived from the testnet simulation. It promotes/demotes the preliminary whitelist from CGD-1465's report based on what landed on chain. Per-row evidence is in `reconciliation.csv` and `fixtures/*.json`.

_demotions_from_preliminary./cosmos.staking.v1beta1.MsgCancelUnbondi: Removed from whitelist — Sei v6.5.0 returns "tx parse error: unable to resolve type URL" at broadcast time. The msg type is NOT registered on Sei's staking module. The indexer's double-credit-at-completion-time risk is moot in practice; no Sei tx can trigger it. Evidence: fixtures/staking-msgcancelunbondingdelegation.json.
_demotions_from_preliminary./cosmos.gov.v1.MsgSubmitProposal: Removed — Sei v6.5.0 returns "tx parse error" on broadcast. Sei is based on cosmos-sdk v0.45 which only ships gov v1beta1. The v1 path is not registered. v1beta1 is whitelisted. Evidence: fixtures/gov-msgsubmitproposal-v1.json.
_demotions_from_preliminary./cosmos.gov.v1.MsgVote: Implicit removal — same v1-not-registered reason. v1beta1.MsgVote remains whitelisted.
_demotions_from_preliminary./cosmos.vesting.v1beta1.MsgCreatePermane: Removed — Sei v6.5.0 mempool silently drops the broadcast (CheckTx accepts, but tx never lands in a block). Matches the report's "0 LCD results" — Sei likely does not register this variant. Evidence: fixtures/vesting-msgcreatepermanentlockedaccount.json.
_demotions_from_preliminary./cosmos.vesting.v1beta1.MsgFundVestingAc: Removed — depends on a pre-existing permanent/periodic account, which Sei doesn't register either.
_demotions_from_preliminary./cosmwasm.wasm.v1.MsgInstantiateContract: Removed — Sei v6.5.0 returns "tx parse error" on broadcast. Sei's wasmd fork predates v0.30 and does not register the predictable-address variant. Evidence: fixtures/wasm-msginstantiatecontract2.json.
_demotions_from_preliminary./seiprotocol.seichain.evm.MsgSend: Moved to historical-mainnet-only — Sei v6.5.0 disallows direct user submission of evm.MsgSend (it's internal-only). Evidence: fixtures/evm-msgsend.json (mainnet historical sample 40CACAF09A15F025...).

_promotions_from_preliminary_needing_fix./cosmos.staking.v1beta1.MsgDelegate: Promoted to whitelist_after_fix — testnet sim confirmed the delegator-side path works (tx 413A59B3... + FA67C0E9...). The SUSPECT verdict (phantom bonded_tokens_pool receive) is contingent on fee-collector map gap — fix that and this is OK.
_promotions_from_preliminary_needing_fix./cosmos.authz.v1beta1.MsgExec: Promoted to whitelist_after_fix — confirmed authz.MsgExec works on Sei testnet (txs 180DCFBA..., 10BE1868..., 49F99605...). Bank-event capture from inner msgs is verified (single + multi inner). The bug is specifically inner-MsgUndelegate parser blindness; fix `parseIsUnstakeTx` and `findVestingMessageIndices` to recurse into MsgExec.msgs[].

msg_dropped_at_sei_mempool: /cosmos.bank.v1beta1.MsgSend with out-of-gas (DeliverTx failures rejected at CheckTx)
_provenance.ibc_client_state_finding: All 51 open transfer-port channels on atlantic-2 had Expired clients at sim time (2026-05-26). MsgTransfer outbound lands at code=29 (Client Expired). MsgTimeout edge case is unobservable on testnet until relayers refresh clients. The historical-mainnet MsgTimeout fixture from the report still stands as the IBC-timeout reference.
